replies.msg:
依幾段
根本就係sided

Hung had felt angry when he passed the wall and saw people putting up posters, as he had been out of work for about two months and believed these were the people who had caused the economic downturn, the website reports.

His lawyer told the judge his income as a tour guide had been badly affected by the protest movement, Radio Television Hong Kong reports.

Judge Kwok Wai-kin agreed during sentencing on Friday that the anti-government protesters had been "like an army", beating people up and blocking roads.

He said protesters who hurt ordinary people while pursuing their cause were no different from terrorists, according to local media reports.

Their extremist conduct was reminiscent of that seen during the Cultural Revolution, the judge is quoted as saying.

quote.msg:
依幾段
根本就係sided

Hung had felt angry when he passed the wall and saw people putting up posters, as he had been out of work for about two months and believed these were the people who had caused the economic downturn, the website reports.

His lawyer told the judge his income as a tour guide had been badly affected by the protest movement, Radio Television Hong Kong reports.

Judge Kwok Wai-kin agreed during sentencing on Friday that the anti-government protesters had been "like an army", beating people up and blocking roads.

He said protesters who hurt ordinary people while pursuing their cause were no different from terrorists, according to local media reports.

Their extremist conduct was reminiscent of that seen during the Cultural Revolution, the judge is quoted as saying.

佢係quoted 個官講咩
唔係BBC自己講喎
正常讀者識自行分析個官有無問題架吓嘛？

睇下上下段嘅分別
將個刀手嘅言論就咁寫出嚟 佢話嬲就話佢嬲
Hung had felt angry when he passed the wall and saw people putting up posters, as he had been out of work for about two months and believed these were the people who had caused the economic downturn, the website reports.

His lawyer told the judge his income as a tour guide had been badly affected by the protest movement, Radio Television Hong Kong reports.


用埋啲明撐嘅verb
Judge Kwok Wai-kin agreed during sentencing on Friday that the anti-government protesters had been "like an army", beating people up and blocking roads.


依兩句咁有立場都夠膽死將個reporter後置
He said protesters who hurt ordinary people while pursuing their cause were no different from terrorists, according to local media reports.

Their extremist conduct was reminiscent of that seen during the Cultural Revolution, the judge is quoted as saying.

replies.msg: Pin 依個會好啲

篇嘢其實無問題，有唔少地方指出法官觀點嘅荒謬：

1 標題已經話：＂用刀插人被判監者得到法官同情＂，呢點違反常理，西方讀者都會覺得奇怪…

2 文章第一句話，法官將示威同軍隊比較，喺經常經歷示威嘅示威者嚟睇，一定覺得法官戇尻，尤其係西方主流媒體都有好多報導，香港示威非常文明克制，呢點可算係既有印象…

3 指出事發地點係＂連儱牆＂，係和平表達意見地方，並非任何激烈衝突嘅現場…

4 指出被告討論後再出手傷人，明顯係因為政見不同而傷人，並非因為受襲反抗或者受到刺激（同佢自稱因為經濟不景而受刺激傷人講法不符，明顯係有預謀）…

5 留意一下文章引號（＂…＂）嘅地方，例如：犯人自稱＂受害人＂，法官形容示威者＂猶如軍隊＂，形容犯人係＂不自願嘅犧牲品，喺血泊之中命懸一線…＂，都係明顯引用法官非常荒謬嘅講法…

篇文一出，忽然咁多P牌出嚟叫人唔好推？大家諗下點解！

歡迎外國巴打用一般西方讀者角度，講下對呢篇文章嘅觀感。
推

quote.msg:
不過篇嘢cite咗好多SCMP嘅report
始終香港英文媒體個pool太細，冇乜SCMP以外嘅source可以做reference

好多香港鬼佬成日投訴話scmp係共狗宣傳機器，但奈何無其他選擇，要知香港事點都要睇，成日都有鬼佬問點解蘋果唔出英文版。

其實如果肥佬黎請，5-7個翻譯做埋英文app+份報紙加8頁英文 summarise 當日重要港聞財經，應該可以搶到scmp唔少市場，而且效果分分鐘好過我哋做文宣
